title: Smoke-Cold Creamy Venezuelan Almond Skillet
description: A rich, creamy, and savory dish featuring almond essence, perfect for a Buddhist temple-friendly brunch. This skillet is packed with flavor and texture, making it a great option for a cold morning.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together almond milk, almond butter, and sugar until smooth.
In a large cast-iron skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
In a medium bowl, whisk together eggs and flour until well combined.
Pour the egg mixture over the melted butter and cook for 2 minutes, until the edges start to set.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika and salt over the top and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Pour the almond milk mixture over the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture starts to simmer and the eggs are almost set.
Stir the mixture gently to distribute the heat evenly.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it sit for 5 minutes, until the eggs are set and the skillet is cold.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top and serve warm.
Garnish with additional parsley, if desired.
Serve immediately, while warm and creamy.
Adjust seasoning as needed, and serve to your guests.
This dish is perfect for a cold morning, as it provides a nice balance of flavors and textures.
This dish can be served as a main course or as a side dish.
This dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, and can be easily adapted to a dairy-free diet, simply by using a non-dairy milk alternative.
